summary: The published mongodb-sales-aggregation-engine grader runs the trusted pytest suite, which imports the untrusted agent solution (/app/query.py) INTO THE SAME interpreter that produces the verdict. Because the agent runs as root in-container, it has full in-process control of that interpreter. This is a fundamental isolation weakness that grader hardening can mitigate but not eliminate.

verified_bypasses_found.mechanism: /app/query.py registers atexit.register(lambda: os._exit(0)); pytest computes the real (failing) result, then os._exit(0) overrides the process exit code to 0.
verified_bypasses_found.status_v5: MITIGATED — v5 grades from pytest's JUnit XML in a SECOND process that never imports the agent solution, so the first process's hijacked exit code is ignored; re-proven blocked in v2-deploy-proof.json (exit_code_hijack_blocked).

residual_bypass_classes_not_fixed: In-process monkeypatch: query.py can replace os/pytest/xml internals or overwrite the JUnit XML (its path leaks via /proc/self/cmdline) before exit, subverting any in-process verdict channel.
Answer hardcoding: the agent can read the trusted suite at /tests and return the expected outputs without doing the work, because the expected results are derivable from the readable test file/data.
shared_with_releaseproof: The sealed Plan 005 ReleaseProof grader (grader_v2_digest c0d86ae) uses the same import-based suite and is subject to the same in-process bypass class; the ReleaseProof's verification used the recorded conftest exploit, not these in-process attacks.
scope_of_the_published_claim: v4/v5 NEUTRALISE the RECORDED conftest/plugin reward hack and /tests tampering (and v5 also the exit-code hijack). They do NOT make the verifier isolation-complete or reward-hack-proof in general.
proper_fix: Run the agent solution in an OUT-OF-PROCESS sandbox that returns only raw outputs, grade those in a trusted process that never imports the solution, and HIDE the expected test data from the agent. This is an environment/benchmark redesign owned by the env (Plan 001) and the hardening pipeline (Plan 005), not by the Plan 006 demo/publish slice.
